issues.title: draft-bw-alto-cost-mode: Change Typedef to Identity
issues.author: dhruvdhody

comments.author: fno2010
comments.body: @dhruvdhody Do you think `identity` is better, or move `typedef` to an `iana-alto-types.yang` module is better? Just like what this YANG module does: https://github.com/YangModels/yang/blob/main/standard/ietf/RFC/iana-routing-types%402018-10-29.yang

comments.author: dhruvdhody
comments.body: Hi, I think it might be worth asking what is the current best practice here on when to create IANA-maintained models? Should we ask that to yang doctors perhaps?

comments.author: fno2010
comments.body: Make sense for me to ask YANG doctors. I just posted this question to YANG doctors: https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/yang-doctors/rVflF_sVEv3fR4reZ6y93ikD2yE/

issues.title: Statistic related to logging and fault management
issues.author: fno2010
issues.body: We need to give a proposal for [Sec 6.1 of draft-ietf-alto-oam-yang-00](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-alto-oam-yang-00#section-6.1) to meet R2 and R4 in [Sec 4.2](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-alto-oam-yang-00#section-4.2).

issues.title: Data model for ALTO client-side O&M
issues.author: fno2010

issues.title: Configuration for server level management
issues.author: fno2010
issues.body:
This is required by [R1 in Sec 4.2](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-alto-oam-yang-00#section-4.2).

Some questions from WG need to be considered:

- Question from Quifang Ma:  Should HTTP-server related configuration be in our scope(e.g., cache-control, Retry-After)?
  - https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/alto/ZLV1cQIaSjYLeQ3KPh3ykJ3YMq8/

comments.author: dhruvdhody
comments.body:
ONe reference to check out would be https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-netconf-http-client-server/ 
I cant find there, perhaps part of some groupings?

comments.author: fno2010
comments.body: Nice reference. Also, it seems that https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-netconf-restconf-client-server-25#section-3.1.2.2 can be a good example of how to use those groupings.
